mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate directly raise the discount rate for equity valuations, mechanically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate increase periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances across a 5-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail auto sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in both series fail to produce a positive return probability above 55 percent over 60 trading days in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth yields negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of monthly observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290.0, persistent inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 290.0 coincides with positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of monthly observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market strength that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to generate a positive return probability above 58 percent over 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate earnings expectations, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth correlates with negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of quarterly observations across a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Declines in industrial production greater than 0.5 percent are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of monthly instances.
